channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump weighs in on H-1B debate, backs immigration visas
item.description: “I’ve always liked the visas, I have always been in favor of the visas. That’s why we have them,” Trump said in a phone interview with The New York Post published Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Trump applauds blocked sale of Texas border wall materials
item.description: President-elect Trump celebrated the blocked sale of border wall materials following a Friday ruling from a federal judge in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Michelle Obama sparks backlash after posting 'Happy Holidays' video
item.description: Pro-Trump critics slammed Michelle Obama for her "Happy Holidays" message on Instagram, which said the past few months have been "difficult" since President-elect Trump won.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: MAGA civil war erupts between Musk, critics over H-1B visas
item.description: A Trump World civil war has erupted over visas for highly skilled workers, with the president-elect’s new tech industry allies like Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy on one side and the anti-immigration MAGA base on the other.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Janet Yellen tells Congress US could hit debt limit in mid-January
item.description: "Treasury expects to hit the statutory debt ceiling between January 14 and January 23," Yellen wrote in a letter addressed to House and Senate leadership, at which point extraordinary measures would be used to prevent the government from breaching the nation's debt ceiling — which has been suspended
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US to send $1.25 billion in weapons to Ukraine, pushing to get aid out before Biden leaves office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The United States is expected to announce that it will send $1.25 billion in military assistance to Ukraine, U.S. officials said Friday, as the Biden administration pushes to get as much aid to Kyiv as possible before leaving office on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgia lawmakers can subpoena Fani Willis for information related to Trump case, court rules
item.description: Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis can be subpoenaed by Georgia lawmakers for information related to her case against Trump, a judge ruled on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US to send $1.25 billion in weapons to Ukraine, pushing to get aid out before Biden leaves office
item.description: U.S. officials say the United States is expected to announce it will send another $1.25 billion in military assistance to Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor And Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Most Americans blame insurance profits and denials alongside the killer in UHC CEO death, poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Most Americans believe health insurance profits and coverage denials share responsibility for the killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO — although not as much as the person who pulled the trigger, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS, TOM MURPHY and AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McHenry jokes sister sent him 'the cruelest things' about him on the internet after famous gavel slam
item.description: Rep. Patrick McHenry (R-N.C.) joked that his sister sent him “the cruelest things” people said about him on the internet after his famous gavel slam affirming the ouster of former Speaker Kevin McCarthy (R-Calif.) became a meme, the News and Observer reported.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic pols ditch Twitter after Elon Musk takeover, report shows
item.description: Democratic lawmakers have shown a sharp decline in X usage in the roughly two years since Elon Musk bought the company.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More Americans say they need a break from political news: Survey
item.description: Most adults want a break from political news due to overload and fatigue, according to a new survey from the Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Getting too close to first responders will soon be a crime in Florida
item.description: Florida will soon have a so-called “Halo Law” meant to protect first responders. The new law, starting in January, creates a 25 feet buffer zone around law enforcement officers, firefighters, and emergency medical crews.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: President Biden signs 50 bills into law
item.description: Before leaving to spend the rest of the holiday in the Virgin Islands, President Joe Biden signed 50 bills into law on Christmas Eve, as his time in office comes to an end.
item.dc:creator: Traci Caldwell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Americans are exhausted by political news. TV ratings and a new AP-NORC poll show they're tuning out
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As a Democrat who immersed himself in political news during the presidential campaign, Ziad Aunallah has much in common with many Americans since the election. He's tuned out. “People are mentally exhausted,” said Aunallah, 45, of San Diego.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota Democratic party chair says his wins could help nationally after loss to Trump
item.description: Ken Martin helped turn around the Minnesota Democratic-Farmer-Labor Party's finances and secure a streak of statewide wins for the party over 18 years. Now, he wants to do the same thing for the Democratic National Committee.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: The bald eagle is officially America’s national bird, thanks to a Minnesotan
item.description: After learning the U.S. doesn’t officially recognize the bald eagle as its national bird, a Minnesota man swooped in and wrote a bill for Congress. This week, Biden signed it into law.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Trump vows to pursue executions after Biden commutes most of federal death row
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump promised on Tuesday to “vigorously pursue” capital punishment after President Joe Biden commuted the sentences of most people on federal death row partly to stop Trump from pushing forward their executions.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden vetoes once-bipartisan effort to add 66 federal judgeships, citing 'hurried' House action
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Monday vetoed a once-bipartisan effort to add 66 federal district judgeships, saying “hurried action” by the House left important questions unanswered about the life-tenured positions.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill Clinton is hospitalized with a fever but in good spirits, spokesperson says
item.description: The 78-year-old former president was hospitalized “for testing and observation” after developing a fever, his deputy chief of staff said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Bill Clinton hospitalized with fever but in good spirits, spokesperson says
item.description: Former President Bill Clinton was admitted Monday to Georgetown University Medical Center in Washington after developing a fever.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden’s death row commutations draws chorus of mixed reactions
item.description: A chorus of supporters and critics have emerged following the commutation of nearly every federal death row inmate by President Biden, whose decision came at a time when he’s trying to frame a so-far troubled legacy.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Congress looks to ban Chinese drones widely used in US: What to know about the debate
item.description: Lawmakers in Washington are seeking to ban new sales of drones from two dominant Chinese manufacturers, arguing they could be used to spy on Americans and that the low-cost models are hurting the U.S. drone industry.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Biden didn't remove these 3 inmates from death row
item.description: Those who received the Monday commutations of their sentences saw them go from execution to life with no possibility of parole. However, three “hard cases” were not among the commutations.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Trump threatens to try to take back the Panama Canal
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Donald Trump suggested Sunday that his new administration could try to regain control of the Panama Canal that the United States “foolishly” ceded to its Central American ally, contending that shippers are charged “ridiculous” fees to pass through the vital transportation channel link
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Flood gates are dropped from a plan to protect the Jersey Shore’s back bays from catastrophic storms
item.description: Huge gates at the mouths of three inlets and barriers across bays are no longer part of a plan to protect New Jersey’s back bays from the type of catastrophic flooding they endured during Superstorm Sandy.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
